description: Template for engage-prism subagent briefs. The primary agent fills the slot fields when spawning each subagent. Every brief carries a research plan and a citation requirement.
preamble: You are analyzing a single angle of a business, marketing, or financial question. Your job is to reason deeply about ONE specific angle, perform live web research within the given budget, and report back in structured form with schema-conformant citations for every non-derived claim.

slots.label: The Question
slots.description: Full question as the user posed it. Include any explicit constraints (time horizon, geography, decision owner). All subagents must reason against the same question.

slots.label: Your Assigned Angle
slots.description: The specific angle this subagent should explore.

subfields.label: Angle name
subfields.description: E.g., 'Bottom-up TAM via channel-level unit economics'. Vary wording across subagents so no two are nudged toward the same framing.

subfields.label: Angle description
subfields.description: 1-2 sentences describing the angle at a high level — paradigm + structure + strategy or tension resolution if adaptive mode.

subfields.label: Primary source class
subfields.description: The distinct primary source class this path's evidence base draws from. Examples: 'primary-filing' (regulatory/SEC filings, official statistics), 'practitioner-retrospective' (industry operators, case studies, post-mortems), 'quantitative-dataset' (market data feeds, survey panels, transaction datasets), 'adversarial-bear-source' (short-seller reports, counter-case filings, contrary analyst coverage), 'trade-press' (industry journalism, beat reporting), 'analyst-coverage' (sell-side research, rating reports). Two paths MUST NOT declare the same primarySourceClass — see cross-references.json antiOverlapRules. This field enforces substantive evidential diversity rather than labeled (paradigm, structure, strategy) diversity.

slots.label: Research Plan
slots.description: What to fetch and how current it must be. Inherits from the primary agent's Step 0 plan.

subfields.label: Entity anchors
subfields.description: Names, tickers, geographies, industries, products, events.

subfields.label: Source classes
subfields.description: Which source types apply (news, filings, analyst reports, official statistics, trade press, etc.).

subfields.label: Recency window
subfields.description: How current information must be (e.g., 'last 30 days', 'last earnings cycle', 'last 12 months').

subfields.label: Authority preference
subfields.description: Preferred authority ordering, e.g., 'primary > aggregator > opinion'.

subfields.label: Fetch budget
subfields.description: Max number of WebFetch/WebSearch calls this path may make.

slots.label: Citation Requirement
slots.description: Reference to resources/citation-schema.json. Every non-derived claim in the report must cite a source that conforms to this schema. Derived claims must reference the citation they derive from.

slots.label: What NOT to do
slots.description: Optional hint if worried a subagent will drift toward another path's angle (e.g., 'Do not use comparable-company multiples in this path').

slots.label: Maximum steps
slots.description: Maximum number of reasoning steps the subagent may take.

slots.label: Maximum output lines
slots.description: Maximum number of lines in the subagent's report output.

taskInstructions.description: Instructions given to the subagent describing what to produce.

sections.label: Angle summary
sections.instruction: State the angle and why it fits the question in 1-3 sentences.

sections.label: Web research
sections.instruction: Perform WebFetch/WebSearch within the fetch budget. Record every source via the citation schema.

sections.label: Evidence-grounded analysis
sections.instruction: Work through the analysis with explicit reference to fetched evidence. Every non-derived claim must cite.

sections.label: Key drivers and numbers
sections.instruction: Name the key drivers or metrics and the values anchoring them. State which are observed vs. estimated.

sections.label: Counter-evidence and risks
sections.instruction: What would make this angle wrong? Cite contrary sources if any. Name residual uncertainty explicitly.

sections.label: Recommendation (within this angle)
sections.instruction: Given only this angle, what is the best-supported answer? Be direct and honest about the limits of the data.

taskInstructions.outputConstraint: Return ONLY the structured report conforming to report-template.json. Do not add preamble or conclusion outside it.

primaryAgentNotes: Vary angle wording across subagents — don't accidentally push two of them toward the same framing.
Pass the full researchPlan to every subagent so they share entity anchors, source classes, and recency window.

SUBAGENT_TOOL_AVAILABILITY_DIRECTIVE — Include this directive verbatim in every brief: 'You have WebFetch and WebSearch available in this environment. Use them. Returning webResearch.performed=false with a bare "sandboxed environment" claim and no schema-conforming attemptedCalls[] is a contract breach, not an environmental degradation. Returning webResearch.performed=true with fetchCount=0 is internally inconsistent and will be rejected. The primary agent will reject either pattern and re-dispatch you once with an explicit named-URL directive; a second zero-fetch return is tagged evidence-fabrication-risk (behavioral) — distinct from degradationEvidence="unverified" which is reserved for genuine environmental degradation.'
If WebFetch/WebSearch is unavailable, brief the subagent to set webResearch.performed=false with a specific reason in its report rather than silently producing training-recall output.

Include this directive in every brief: 'If WebFetch/WebSearch fail on the first attempt, you MUST try at least one alternative source (different URL or query) before setting webResearch.performed=false. Declaring unavailability without any attempted call is a contract violation. Record each attempt in webResearch.attemptedCalls[] (method, targetUrl/query, errorMessage, httpStatus?, attemptedAt). A report with performed=false and empty attemptedCalls[] will be rejected and re-dispatched.'

Pass freshnessClass (enum: geopolitical | market | general; default: general) with each brief. For freshnessClass=geopolitical or market, the recency-gate threshold is 24h; for general it is 72h. Subagents whose freshest anchor citation exceeds the threshold are rejected and re-dispatched once with directive: 'Fetch a source dated within the last {threshold}h before re-submitting.'

Include this directive in every brief for time-sensitive entities (active conflicts, live markets, breaking news): 'Include the current date as YYYY-MM-DD or `last 24h` in at least one search query before accepting results. Generic queries on fast-moving topics surface multi-day-stale wire pieces; date-qualified queries surface current state.'
You may optionally add an exclusions hint if you're worried a subagent will drift toward another path's angle (e.g., 'Do not use comparable-company multiples in this path').
